interactions.summary: Jiang opens the semester by naming three live conflicts: Ukraine, the Middle East, and a possible Iran-centered escalation that could draw in Russia, China, India, and East Asian energy interests.

He defines the third conflict as the United States against the world: Pax Americana created prosperity by protecting trade but also created resentment through unipolar military and economic dominance.
He states the course method: enough history should let students understand current events and predict future movement, then introduces the older cyclical model of history.

claims.claim: Jiang identifies Ukraine, the Middle East, and the United States against the world as the three major conflicts framing the 2025 semester.
He predicts that the Middle East conflict will spread because an Israel-Iran war would threaten oil supply, the Strait of Hormuz, and East Asian economies, forcing major powers to intervene.
claims.temporal_scope: Prediction and geopolitical model stated on 2025-02-18.

claims.claim: Pax Americana brought prosperity by securing trade routes but also produced conflict because the United States could destroy countries without effective restraint.
claims.temporal_scope: Historical diagnosis of the post-1991 unipolar moment.

claims.claim: Jiang treats history as useful only if it can explain the present and help predict what will happen next.
The cyclical model understands historical development through repeating patterns such as seasons, lives, and Chinese dynastic rise and decline.
claims.temporal_scope: Definition of a historical model in this lecture.

signature_moments.moment: Iran becomes the linchpin of the global economy.
signature_moments.source_phrase: Iran right now, is the linchpin of the global economy
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It turns a regional conflict into the hinge of world-system escalation.

signature_moments.moment: The American peace is both the condition of Chinese growth and the source of global resentment.
signature_moments.source_phrase: China would not have grown as fast as China did without the Pax Americana
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It preserves the double edge in Jiang's view of hegemony.

signature_moments.moment: History should predict, not merely remember.
signature_moments.source_phrase: you will also be able to predict what will happen
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It states the epistemic demand behind the whole episode.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: The American peace: a post-Soviet unipolar order in which the United States protects trade and dictates economic policy while also exercising destructive military power.
A model in which history moves like seasons or dynasties: rise, decline, rebellion, renewal, and repetition.

chronology_notes.note: The lecture is dated 2025-02-18 and explicitly opens the second semester by connecting the course to ongoing wars and predictions.

uncertainty_notes.note: ASR likely garbles one clause around Israel and Syria, but the intended point is that Israel is engaged across multiple neighboring fronts.
